statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided through stdin.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a boolean indicating whether the array is a max heap.
statement.notes: Consider space and time complexity when solving this problem.

examples.explanation: The array [5, 3, 8] is a max heap.

hints.text: You should use a bottom-up approach (heapify up) to maintain the heap property after insertion.

editorial.title: Fix Heap Insertion
editorial.intuition: To fix the heap insertion bug, we need to ensure that the newly inserted element is correctly placed at the correct position after insertion.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute-force approach would be to rebuild the entire heap after each insertion, which would lead to an inefficient solution.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a bottom-up approach (heapify up) to maintain the heap property after insertion. This approach ensures that the heap property is maintained after each insertion.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a bottom-up approach (heapify up) and maintaining the heap property after insertion. This approach ensures that the heap remains a max heap after each insertion.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of the solution by showing that the heap property is maintained after each insertion using the bottom-up approach (heapify up).
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution can help in understanding the approach and identifying any potential issues.
commonMistakes: Not using the bottom-up approach (heapify up) to maintain the heap property after insertion.
Not correctly placing the newly inserted element at the correct position after insertion.
